title: Representation and Operations on Polynomials
description: A visual breakdown of how polynomials can be represented in memory using different data structures (Array and Linked List) and the common operations performed on them.
relationships: A Polynomial Expression can be stored in memory as either an Array (for dense polynomials) or a Linked List (for sparse polynomials). These data structures then enable core operations like Addition, Evaluation, and Multiplication to be performed efficiently.
